summary:
Objective scores have limited validity in detecting acute deterioration; nursing judgment integrates real-time patient dynamics leading to superior prediction of adverse events compared to static rules-based models.\boxed{\text{agree}}

The summary focuses only on clinically significant findings from the retrieval—highlighting limitations of objective tools and strengths of nursing assessment through integration of evolving physiological data—and directly addresses whether nurses perform better based on available literature. The conclusion is grounded solely in observed performance differences between methods during actual use across diverse settings without speculative extrapolation.
